steps.title: Identify the theory gap
steps.content: Text 2 reveals that the script's symbols functioned as both words and syllables depending on context—a dual capacity that Lacadena's syllable-focused theory missed.

steps.title: Apply to the characterization
steps.content:
D is correct: this choice directly names what Lacadena missed—that the script could represent whole words, not just syllables.
A misrepresents Lacadena's theory; B and C invoke explanations absent from Text 2.
That matches Choice D — By cautioning that the theory overlooks certain important aspects of how the script functioned.
